key_insight: WarpagePINN predicts chiplet temperature and thermal warpage with a two-stage physics-informed neural network that avoids labeled simulation data by embedding the governing physics directly into training.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a fast surrogate for advanced packaging design, enabling rapid warpage assessment and CTE sensitivity studies without repeated finite element runs.
method: Stage 1: Fourier-series temperature model that satisfies boundary conditions and is trained from the PDE residual only.
Stage 2: MLP warpage model trained with a hybrid energy-based supervisory loss instead of residual loss.
Parametric extension quantifies uncertainty and variation from CTE mismatch.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports excellent agreement with finite element results, with MAE around 0.2 μm and about 1000× speedup in CTE parameterization studies.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of physics assumptions and boundary/constitutive modeling, so transfer to different package layouts or material systems may require retraining and careful validation.
paper_title: WarpagePINN: Thermal Warpage Prediction in Advanced Packaging via a Two-Stage Physics-Informed Neural Networks
